pr.title: PR A — Local Runner: execution_chain_run + capability_evidence_apply loop actions
pr.description:
## Summary

Phase 1 enabler: teach the **Local Runner** to *perform the loop*, not just run test/build/deploy. Adds two allowlisted actions so the substrate loop (canonical chain → evidence bundle → promotion/confidence) can be driven by the runner and produce a captured evidence bundle — the thing that turns Windows evidence from *attested* into *captured*.

Two new actions in `tools/local_runner/local_runner.py`:
- **`execution_chain_run`** → fixed command `poetry run axiom execution-chain-run --json-output`. Drives one deterministic capability through Plan → Step → Attempt(v2) → Result → Artifact → Evidence → Report, writing `artifacts/execution_chain/<run>/evidence.json`.
- **`capability_evidence_apply`** → applies the newest chain evidence bundle to capability state. The `--evidence` path is **built at run time** by the runner and sandbox-validated, not taken from the task file:

```python
# _resolve_latest_evidence(workspace):
chain_dir = Path(workspace).resolve() / "artifacts" / "execution_chain"
evidence  = newest(chain_dir.glob("*/evidence.json"))   # by mtime
assert ws_root == evidence or ws_root in evidence.parents  # no escape
# -> poetry run axiom capability-evidence-apply --evidence <evidence>
```

This preserves the runner's existing security model (named actions only, **no arbitrary args/paths** from `task.json`). If no bundle exists yet, the action is **blocked** with guidance to run `execution_chain_run` first (not a hard error).

Also gitignores the three generated artifact dirs these actions produce (`artifacts/execution_chain/`, `artifacts/capability_evidence_intake/`, `artifacts/capability_confidence/`) so runs never get committed — satisfies the "only gitignored artifact folders are produced" Windows-revalidation rule.

## What did NOT change
- No change to the execution chain, evidence, or promotion **semantics** — this only *invokes* existing CLI commands. No new framework, no CLI command added, no canonical-path change. Existing runner actions and security checks untouched.

## Tests run (lane 1 / Ubuntu)
- `poetry run pytest tests/test_local_runner.py -q` → **52 passed, 1 skipped**. New `TestExecutionChainLoopActions` covers registration, static vs run-time command shape, newest-bundle selection, sandbox-escape rejection, and the blocked-without-bundle path.
- `ruff check` clean on changed files.
- **End-to-end smoke on Ubuntu:** ran both actions through the runner CLI — `execution_chain_run` produced `artifacts/execution_chain/<uuid>/evidence.json`; `capability_evidence_apply` resolved that exact bundle and wrote the confidence/intake update. Full substrate loop closes off-Windows.

## Validation still pending
- **Windows (lane 2):** these actions must be exercised in the 8-point post-#151 Windows revalidation on `C:\Dev\Axiom\Code\Axiom-platform`. Not yet run.
- **Real Revit (lane 3):** unaffected here — this is the deterministic (non-Revit) chain. Real SetParameterValue-via-pipe remains pending furnace evidence (PR C).

## Factory relevance
Directly strengthens the verification factory: makes the loop runnable by the Local Runner and produces a captured evidence bundle + confidence update. No Revit live validation required; 2024 baseline unaffected.


Link to Devin session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/8ad1c9ac0d2f4252bb77647f1eabaac7
Requested by: @plamen-dev
pr.author: plamen-dev

commits.author: plamen.hristov@imsdevlab.onmicrosoft.com
commits.message:
Local Runner: add execution_chain_run + capability_evidence_apply loop actions

Enables the runner to drive the canonical execution chain (Plan->...->Evidence->Report) and apply the resulting evidence bundle to capability state (promotion/confidence). capability-evidence-apply resolves the newest chain evidence bundle from a runner-derived, sandbox-validated path (no task-supplied path), preserving the no-arbitrary-args security model. Gitignores the three generated artifact dirs.

Co-Authored-By: Devin AI <158243242+devin-ai-integration[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
